meaning: n.鍠濆僵,娆㈠懠v.娆㈠懠,绉拌禐
examples: He received acclaim as a champion of the oppressed.
Elgar received many honours and much acclaim and yet he often considered himself a failure.
In her work, she revealed a side of nature so exotic, dramatic and valuable to Europeans of the time that she received much acclaim.
There was bound to be some pushback in the face of all that acclaim.
The brothers quickly earned acclaim in their own right, and were rewarded well for their labors.
